title: digitalis × di-tellae
intro: Digitalis × di-tellae, also known as the hybrid foxglove, is a rare and sterile hybrid plant resulting from the cross-pollination of Digitalis purpurea and Digitalis ferruginea. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its ability to increase the force of the heart's contractions and improve heart function. The health benefits of Digitalis × di-tellae include its potential to treat heart failure, atrial fibrillation, and other cardiac conditions. Herbal preparations of Digitalis × di-tellae are typically made from the leaves and flowers, which can be dried, powdered, or processed into capsules or tinctures, although its use requires careful dosing and medical supervision due to its potency.
